description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who must package and integrity-check a small, three-file
dataset that already exists on the workstation.

Dataset location
/home/user/datasets/sentiment140/

Present files (already on disk – do NOT recreate them)
1. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part1.txt
2. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part2.txt
3. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part3.txt

Goal
Create an archive, produce a SHA-256 manifest, and generate a human-readable verification log.

Sub-tasks
A. Archive  
   • Create /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/sentiment140_parts.tar.gz that contains ONLY the
     three text files listed above.  
   • Inside the archive the files must be located under a single directory called
     sentiment140_parts/ (e.g. sentiment140_parts/train_part1.txt, …).

B. Check-sum manifest  
   • Generate /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/checksums.sha256  
   • Format requirements (must match exactly – the automated test will parse the file byte-for-byte):
     1. One line per artefact, four lines total.  
     2. Alphabetical order by the relative path used in the second column:
        sentiment140_parts.tar.gz
        train_part1.txt
        train_part2.txt
        train_part3.txt
        (That is the exact order expected in the manifest.)  
     3. Each line MUST follow the canonical output of “sha256sum” (two spaces between digest and
        path, newline terminated). Example template (length of digest must be 64 hex
        characters):
        f5d1278e8109edd94e1e4197e04873b9fcfc4b0fe4aeb432d16a44d8e5e29e89␠␠sentiment140_parts.tar.gz\n
        …  
     4. The second column must be the path relative to /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/, **NOT**
        an absolute path.

C. Verification log  
   • Run an integrity check that reads the manifest and verifies each file.  
   • Capture the verifier’s stdout stream **verbatim** into
     /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/verification.log.  
   • The log must therefore contain exactly four lines in this format (again, canonical output
     of “sha256sum -c”):  
       sentiment140_parts.tar.gz: OK  
       train_part1.txt: OK  
       train_part2.txt: OK  
       train_part3.txt: OK  
     Each line has a trailing newline and nothing else – no summary line, no extra spaces.

Additional constraints & hints
• At least the three individual text files must have their checksums calculated in
  parallel (for example, by backgrounding commands or by using xargs -P).  
• You do **not** need root privileges; everything happens in the user’s home directory.  
• The task is complete when the archive, the manifest, and the verification log all exist
  exactly as specified and the verification log shows “OK” for all four artefacts.

Deliverables to be present at the end
1. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/sentiment140_parts.tar.gz  
2. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/checksums.sha256  
3. /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/verification.log
truth:
Initial dataset files and their exact contents (lines are terminated by ‘\n’ and the files
contain no extra blank line at the end):

File: /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part1.txt
I love this movie\tpositive
It was great!\tpositive

File: /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part2.txt
I hate this movie\tnegative
It was terrible!\tnegative

File: /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/train_part3.txt
It was okay\tneutral
Not bad, not good\tneutral

Pre-task file permissions
• Directory /home/user/datasets/sentiment140/ exists and is writable by the regular user.
• All three text files are readable/writable by the regular user (0644).

Ground-truth validation checklist the automated tests will enforce
1. The archive sentiment140_parts.tar.gz exists and, when listed with “tar -tf”, contains
   exactly:
     sentiment140_parts/train_part1.txt
     sentiment140_parts/train_part2.txt
     sentiment140_parts/train_part3.txt
   with byte-for-byte identical contents to the original files.
2. The manifest checksums.sha256 exists, has four lines, is sorted exactly as specified,
   and each SHA-256 digest matches the current content of the referenced file (the test
   script will recompute digests to confirm correctness).
3. The verification.log file exists and contains exactly the four “: OK” lines in the
   order shown in the public task description, with no extra lines or whitespace.
4. The manifest file uses two spaces (0x20 0x20) to separate each digest from its
   relative path.
5. All three checksum calculations for the *individual* text files must have been issued
   concurrently (the harness checks that the agent started at least two checksum
   commands before any one of them finished).
